q1.prompt: Which of the following describes the molecular mechanism of action for linezolid?

options.label: Prevention of polypeptide elongation at the 30S ribosome
Inhibition of peptidyl transferase at the 50S subunit
Structural analogy to p-aminobenzoic acid in folate synthesis
Inhibition of peptide bond formation at the 50S subunit
Prevention of 70S initiation complex formation at the 50S subunit

q2.prompt: What is a significant clinical risk associated with the administration of sulfonamides to neonates?

options.label: Inability to distinguish red from green colours
Permanent damage to developing cartilage and tendons
Reversible bone growth inhibition and tooth discoloration
Kernicterus resulting from bilirubin displacement from albumin

q3.prompt: What is the molecular basis for methicillin resistance in Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA)?

options.label: Acquisition of the mecA gene encoding an altered PBP2a
Mutation in the rpoB gene encoding RNA polymerase
Synthesis of a dipeptide with reduced affinity for glycopeptides
Production of β-lactamase enzymes that cleave the drug

q4.prompt: Which bacteriostatic antibiotic inhibits peptidyl transferase at the 50S subunit but is restricted in use due to the risk of aplastic anemia and Gray Baby Syndrome?

q5.prompt: A 12-year-old patient with cystic fibrosis is diagnosed with a pulmonary infection caused by Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Which of the following "CAMPFIRE" mnemonic drugs treats this infection by inhibiting bacterial cell wall synthesis?

q6.prompt: Which antibacterial agent exerts its bactericidal effect by binding to the β-subunit of DNA-dependent RNA polymerase to prevent transcription?

q7.prompt: For which of the following reasons are tetracyclines, such as doxycycline, generally avoided in children under 8 years of age?

options.label: High risk of acute hemolytic anemia in G6PD deficiency
Risk of Gray Baby Syndrome due to lack of liver enzymes
Risk of permanent tooth discoloration and bone growth inhibition
Potential for permanent damage to developing cartilage
Structural displacement of bilirubin causing kernicterus

q8.prompt: Which of the following antibacterial agents is used topically to treat impetigo and acts by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis?

q9.prompt: Which route of administration is specifically indicated for vancomycin when used to treat pseudomembranous colitis caused by Clostridioides difficile?

q10.prompt: A patient with Epstein-Barr Virus (EBV) infectious mononucleosis develops a diffuse maculopapular rash after being treated for secondary pharyngitis. Which of the following drugs is most likely responsible?
